info.name: Recluse
info.description: The Recluse class based on the Recluse from Elden Ring Nightreign.

description.p: Innate magic courses through them, yet they never learned to hone it properly. A Recluse knows magic like a normal person would breath, yet their lifes lived alone for so many years never gave them the ability to properly utalize magic. What a Wizard might call a Fireball, the Recluse would see it as fire energy, nothing more than an element to control rather than the manipulation of the Weave.
Though they don't quite grasp magic itself, a Recluse is an expert in grasping a spells elemental energy, harnissing it and even mixing it up like a cocktail to suit their needs in their lonely life.

A Recluses life is spent alone, away from cities and villages, maybe in the mountains or a forest, maybe even in a nice field. They are frail, yet know how to survive with their swiftness and survival knowledge.

tr.td: Choose 3: Arcana, Investigation, Nature, Religion, Perception or Survival

table.tr: Tool ProficienciesHerbalism Kit, Alchemist's Supplies, Cook's Utensils

tr.td: Choose A or B: (A) 2 Rations, Quarterstaff, Herbalism Kit, 22 GP; or (B) 70 GP

ul.li: Gain all the traits in the Core Recluse Traits table.
Gain the Recluse's level 1 features which are listed in the Recluse Features table.
Gain the following traits from the Core Recluse Traits table: Hit Point Die, proficiency in one skill featured in the Core Recluse Traits table and proficiency with Alchemist's Supplies.
Gain the Recluse's level 1 features which are listed in the Recluse Features table.

description.p: As a Recluse, you gain the following class features when you reach specified Recluse levels. These features are listed in the Recluse Features table.

setters.set: A lonesome life with innate, untrained Magic
one skill of your choice from Acana, Nature or Survival, Alchemist's Supplies

description.p: You learned to sense the resedue magic left in creatures hit by magical forces, absorbing it before it fades completely and mixing it into a powerful cocktail of magic.
Mixing Elements. You are able to absorb a maximum of up to 3 elements. The Magic Cocktails Table below shows what each combination of Elements achieve what Cocktail.
You can absorb an element 2 times per turn. Once as a Free Action, and the second time as a Bonus Action.

If a Creature other than you recieves damage that is either Force, Fire, Lightning or Radiant, that Element lingers on them until the end of your next turn. (For example, if a creature is hit by an attack or spell that deals fire damage, the Fire Element will stay on that creature until you end your turn. This can happen before, while or after your turn).

A Creature can only have one Element lingering at a time. If a Creature is hit by another absorbable Element, the old Element will be replaced by the new one. (For example, if a creature is hit by an attack that deals radiant damage while the Fire Element was lingering on it, the Fire Element will be replaced by the Radiant Element).

sheet.description: You can absorb one Element as a Free Action, you can absorb a second Element that turn as a Bonus Action. A Creature only holds one Element at a time.

description.p: You have an innate sense of magic, but it is somewhat limited.
Cantrips. You know the unique Fire Cantrip, Lightning Cantrip, Force Cantrip and Radiant Cantrip Cantrips of the Recluse.

Your Cantrips evolve as you gain Recluse Levels. Once at Level 10, and again at Level 20. Cantrips you gain through a Recluse Subclass have their own pace of evolution that is explained in the Subclass Features.
Spellcasting Ability. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your Recluse spells.
Spellcasting Focus. Your magic is innate, making you not needing a specific Spellcasting Focus.

center.p: Spell save DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence modifier
Spell attack modifier = your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence modifier

sheet.description: You don't need a Spellcasting Focus to cast your Recluse spells.

description.p: You know your body is weak, that's why you learned to sharpen your instincts with magic to the point where danger rarely strikes you.

When you are hit by a weapon attack or a spell that damages you through a spell attack (For example Toll of the Dead, but not Fireball), but before the damage is calculated, you can use your reaction to have that attack miss you instead.
You can use this Feature a number of times equal to your proficiency modifier and you regain all uses at the end of a Long Rest.

sheet.description: If you get hit by an attack or spell that is a ranged/melee spell attack, don't get hit instead.

description.p: You gain Expertise in two of your skill proficiencies of your choice. Perception and Arcana are recommended if you have proficiency in them.
At Recluse level 9, you gain Expertise in two more of your skill proficiencies of your choice.
Your long time of surviving alone comes with huge benefits.
You have advantage on Intelligence (Nature) and Wisdom (Survival) checks when adventuring in areas that are barren of Civilization. (For example Forests, Swamps, Tundras or Mountains)

sheet.description: You have advantage at Intelligence (Nature) and Wisdom (Survival) checks while in areas without any civilization.

description.p: Your talent in manifesting the worlds elements manifests deeper with experience.

You gain a Recluse Subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Recluse levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Recluse level or lower.
You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Recluse levels 8, 12, and 16.
This Feature works like the Extra Attack Class Feature.

You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn. You may replace one of your attacks with a Cantrip you know of the Recluse Spell List. When you use the Magic action to cast a Cantrip, you may cast a second Cantrip of your choice from the Recluse Spell List.

sheet.description: You can attack twice instead of once. You can replace one of your attacks with a Cantrip of the Recluse Spell List. On the Magic action, when casting a Cantrip, you can cast a second Cantrip of the Recluse Spell List.

description.p: Your experience in merging elements causes you to unlock effects you didn't know about before.
Potentcy. You unlock the Potent effects of your Magic Cocktail spells.

sheet.description: Your Magic Cocktail Spells now use the Potent effects when casting them.

description.p: When you roll Initiative, you can add your Proficiency Bonus to the roll. Additionally, the first attack made against you from a creature that acts before you in the Initiative Order is made with disadvantage. This feature triggers only once per Round.

sheet.description: The first Attack you receive from a creature that's placed before you in the Initiative is made with disadvantage.

description.p: A song of Blood and Soul, warm as it is cold. Sing a Song unknown to make their health your own.

Soulblood Song. As an action, you sing a song deep inside your soul, marking every hostile creature in a 40 ft. Radius. Loose 3d6 Hit Points when using this feature, then, until the end of your third turn after using this feature (Counting the turn you use it), whenever you or an ally damage a marked creature, the person who damaged it heals for 1d6 Hit Points. Once used, you cannot use this feature until you finish a Long Rest.

sheet.description: Action: Take 3d6 Damage. Mark every hostile creature in a 40 ft. Radius around you. Hitting a marked creature heals you or the ally who hit it for 1d6. Lasts until the end of your 3rd turn (Including the turn you use Bloodsoul Song).

description.p: You honed your Elemental Prowess, empowering your Cantrips in a way you didn't think possible.

Your Main Cantrips Fire, Force, Lightning and Radiant are replaced by Fire2, Force2, Lightning2 and Radiant2. These new Cantrips are upgraded Forms of the previous Cantrips, dealing more damage or having better general effects.
You learned to react even faster when dodging a foe.
When using your reaction to use Meek Dodge, you can now cast a Cantrip against the attacking creature as part of the same reaction.

sheet.description: When using Meek Dodge, you can cast a Cantrip against the attacking creature as part of the same reaction.

description.p: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. Boon of Spell Irresistible Offense or Boon of Truesight is recommended.

You have mastered the control over your innate Magic. Your Main Cantrips Fire2, Force2, Lightning and Radiant2 evolve again, becoming Fire3, Force3, Lightning3 and Radiant3, boosting their damage or general effects to the upmost.
Additionally, whenever you cast your cocktail spell, you may keep one of the three elements used in your cocktail for the new cocktail.

sheet.description: Whenever you cast your Cocktail, you may keep one of the elements used to cast it for the next Cocktail.
